title: helianthemum grandiflorum subsp. glabrum
intro: Helianthemum grandiflorum subsp. glabrum, also known as the alpine rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cistaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The alpine rockrose has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, providing antioxidant and antimicrobial benefits, and has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as arthritis, bronchitis, and digestive issues. The alpine rockrose is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically in the form of a salve or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
